24.reason: Missing NULL pointer check; if p is NULL, accessing p->left causes segmentation fault.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing NULL pointer check; if p is NULL, accessing p->left causes segmentation fault.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing NULL check for p: if p is NULL (e.g., empty tree or called with a NULL child), this dereference causes a segmentation fault. The reference program checks if(p!=NULL) first.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing NULL check for pointer p causes segmentation fault when recursively calling search on NULL children.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing null pointer check for p before accessing p->left and p->right, which causes a segmentation fault when p is NULL

29.reason: [Potentially calls search with a NULL pointer if p->left is NULL, leading to a crash. Should only call if p->left != NULL, or add a NULL check at the beginning of search.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Potentially calls search with a NULL pointer if p->left is NULL, leading to a crash. Should only call if p->left != NULL, or add a NULL check at the beginning of search.]

30.reason: [Same issue as line 30: can pass NULL if p->right is NULL.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue as line 30: can pass NULL if p->right is NULL.]

42.reason: [The function 'search' may dereference a NULL pointer when the tree is empty (n=0), causing undefined behavior. Also, the height tracking logic using global variable 'num' is flawed because it doesn't correctly track depth during traversal — it increments before recursing but doesn't account for separate paths properly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The function 'search' may dereference a NULL pointer when the tree is empty (n=0), causing undefined behavior. Also, the height tracking logic using global variable 'num' is flawed because it doesn't correctly track depth during traversal — it increments before recursing but doesn't account for separate paths properly.]
